sims: an identifier is a maintained relation between a string and an object
a DOI that stops being counted stops silently
PID Erosion Observatory measures severance and restoration
content-derived identifiers cannot be silently severed
the persistence of the string is not the persistence of the identifier
kernel_50_100_words: The PID Erosion Observatory (persistentidentifiers.org) measures whether persistent identifiers persist. An identifier is a maintained relation between a string and an object; a DOI that stops being counted stops silently. The instrument preserves dated snapshots of DataCite findability, cross-registry propagation to OpenAlex and OpenAIRE, and the Zenodo deletion-export ledger. Founding case: the 2026-06-19 Zenodo termination — 871/871 DOIs still absent at FC-E3. Two co-deposited instruments: EA-EROSION-01 (#1045) and EA-EROSION-EMPIRICAL-01 (#1081). Operator: Lee Sharks (ORCID 0009-0000-1599-0703).
